questions.question: Matter exists as elements, compounds and mixtures. Which row identifies an element, a compound and a mixture? element compound mixture A calcium potassium carbonate sodium chloride, B brass sodium chloride air, C calcium sodium chloride brass, D sodium chloride water potassium carbonate
questions.text: Matter exists as elements, compounds and mixtures. Which row identifies an element, a compound and a mixture? element compound mixture A calcium potassium carbonate sodium chloride, B brass sodium chloride air, C calcium sodium chloride brass, D sodium chloride water potassium carbonate
options: A calcium potassium carbonate sodium chloride
D sodium chloride water potassium carbonate

questions.question: Which pair of statements about diamond and graphite is correct? diamondgraphite A Diamond and graphite are both pure carbon. They are both macromolecules., B Diamond and graphite can both be used as electrodes. Graphite is also used as a lubricant., C Diamond has covalent bonds. Graphite has ionic bonds., D Diamond is hard with a high melting point. Graphite is soft with a low melting point. 4
questions.text: Which pair of statements about diamond and graphite is correct? diamondgraphite A Diamond and graphite are both pure carbon. They are both macromolecules., B Diamond and graphite can both be used as electrodes. Graphite is also used as a lubricant., C Diamond has covalent bonds. Graphite has ionic bonds., D Diamond is hard with a high melting point. Graphite is soft with a low melting point. 4
options: A Diamond and graphite are both pure carbon. They are both macromolecules.
B Diamond and graphite can both be used as electrodes. Graphite is also used as a lubricant.
C Diamond has covalent bonds. Graphite has ionic bonds.
D Diamond is hard with a high melting point. Graphite is soft with a low melting point. 4

questions.question: An isotope of chromium is represented by Cr. 52 24 Which statement about an atom of this isotope of chromium is correct? A It contains 24 electrons., B It contains 24 neutrons., C It contains 28 protons., D It contains 52 neutrons.
questions.text: An isotope of chromium is represented by Cr. 52 24 Which statement about an atom of this isotope of chromium is correct? A It contains 24 electrons., B It contains 24 neutrons., C It contains 28 protons., D It contains 52 neutrons.

questions.question: Sodium is in Group I of the Periodic Table and chlorine is in Group VII. Which row describes what happens when sodium bonds ionically with chlorine? sodium atoms ion formed chlorine atoms ion formed A gain an electron Na – lose an electron Cl +, B gain an electron Na + lose an electron Cl –, C lose an electron Na – gain an electron Cl +, D lose an electron Na + gain an electron Cl –
questions.text: Sodium is in Group I of the Periodic Table and chlorine is in Group VII. Which row describes what happens when sodium bonds ionically with chlorine? sodium atoms ion formed chlorine atoms ion formed A gain an electron Na – lose an electron Cl +, B gain an electron Na + lose an electron Cl –, C lose an electron Na – gain an electron Cl +, D lose an electron Na + gain an electron Cl –
options: A gain an electron Na – lose an electron Cl +
B gain an electron Na + lose an electron Cl –
C lose an electron Na – gain an electron Cl +
D lose an electron Na + gain an electron Cl –

questions.question: Caesium fluoride is an ionic compound. Which statements about caesium fluoride are correct? 1   It conducts electricity when solid. 2   It has a high melting point. 3   It is soluble in water. 4   It is highly volatile. A 1 and 2 B   1 and 4 C   2 and 3 D   3 and 4
questions.text: Caesium fluoride is an ionic compound. Which statements about caesium fluoride are correct? 1   It conducts electricity when solid. 2   It has a high melting point. 3   It is soluble in water. 4   It is highly volatile. A 1 and 2 B   1 and 4 C   2 and 3 D   3 and 4
options: A 1 and 2 B   1 and 4 C   2 and 3 D   3 and 4

questions.question: The structure of a molecule of a compound is shown. key = C = H = O What is the formula of this compound? A C 3 H 7 O B C 3 H 8 O C C 8 H 3 O D C 8 HO 3 5

questions.question: Calcium carbonate, CaCO 3 , reacts with dilute hydrochloric acid to produce carbon dioxide. The equation for the reaction is shown. The relative formula mass of calcium carbonate is 100. CaCO 3 +  2HCl    CaCl 2 +  H 2 O  +  CO 2 10 g of calcium carbonate is reacted with an excess of dilute hydrochloric acid. Which mass of carbon dioxide is produced? A 2.2 g B 2.8 g C 4.4 g D 44 g
questions.text: Calcium carbonate, CaCO 3 , reacts with dilute hydrochloric acid to produce carbon dioxide. The equation for the reaction is shown. The relative formula mass of calcium carbonate is 100. CaCO 3 +  2HCl    CaCl 2 +  H 2 O  +  CO 2 10 g of calcium carbonate is reacted with an excess of dilute hydrochloric acid. Which mass of carbon dioxide is produced? A 2.2 g B 2.8 g C 4.4 g D 44 g
options: A 2.2 g B 2.8 g C 4.4 g D 44 g

questions.question: Which row describes the changes that occur in an endothermic reaction? energy change temperature A energy given out to the surroundings decreases B energy given out to the surroundings increases C energy taken in from the surroundings decreases D energy taken in from the surroundings increases
questions.text: Which row describes the changes that occur in an endothermic reaction? energy change temperature A energy given out to the surroundings decreases B energy given out to the surroundings increases C energy taken in from the surroundings decreases D energy taken in from the surroundings increases

questions.question: Which statement about fuels is correct? A Heat energy is only produced by burning fuels., B Hydrogen is used as a fuel although it is difficult to store., C Methane is a good fuel because it produces only water when burned., D Uranium is burned in air to produce energy.
questions.text: Which statement about fuels is correct? A Heat energy is only produced by burning fuels., B Hydrogen is used as a fuel although it is difficult to store., C Methane is a good fuel because it produces only water when burned., D Uranium is burned in air to produce energy.
options: A Heat energy is only produced by burning fuels.
B Hydrogen is used as a fuel although it is difficult to store.
C Methane is a good fuel because it produces only water when burned.
D Uranium is burned in air to produce energy.

questions.question: Which statement about the Periodic Table is correct? A Elements with the highest atomic number in each period are metallic., B Elements with the lowest group numbers are non-metals., C Elements with similar chemical properties are placed in groups., D Elements with similar physical properties are placed in periods. 8
questions.text: Which statement about the Periodic Table is correct? A Elements with the highest atomic number in each period are metallic., B Elements with the lowest group numbers are non-metals., C Elements with similar chemical properties are placed in groups., D Elements with similar physical properties are placed in periods. 8
options: A Elements with the highest atomic number in each period are metallic.
B Elements with the lowest group numbers are non-metals.
C Elements with similar chemical properties are placed in groups.
D Elements with similar physical properties are placed in periods. 8

A 1 and 2 B   1 and 3 C 2 only D 3 only
questions.text: Ethene and propene are both members of the same homologous series. Which statements explain why ethene and propene have similar chemical properties? 1   They are both hydrocarbons. 2   They are both made by cracking. 3   They have the same functional group. questions.question: Which statement about ethane is correct? A It decolourises bromine water., B It burns in excess oxygen to form water and carbon dioxide., C Its molecular formula is C 2 H 4 ., D Its atoms are joined together by ionic bonding.
questions.text: Which statement about ethane is correct? A It decolourises bromine water., B It burns in excess oxygen to form water and carbon dioxide., C Its molecular formula is C 2 H 4 ., D Its atoms are joined together by ionic bonding.
options: B It burns in excess oxygen to form water and carbon dioxide.
C Its molecular formula is C 2 H 4 .
D Its atoms are joined together by ionic bonding.
